payload.key_insight: JPMorgan's CIB segment reported exceptionally strong Markets revenue (+17% YoY) with Equity Markets surging +40% driven by Prime, and Fixed Income up +7%, signaling robust industry-wide trading conditions that should benefit Goldman Sachs's dominant FICC and Equities franchises. However, Investment Banking fees declined -5% across all products at JPM, suggesting muted near-term IB fee recovery may temper GS IB revenue estimates.
specific_quotes: Equity Markets revenue was $2.9 billion, up 40%, driven by higher revenue across products, particularly in Prime.
Fixed Income Markets revenue was $5.4 billion, up 7%, driven by strong performance in Securitized Products, Rates and Currencies & Emerging Markets, largely offset by lower revenue in Credit.
Investment Banking fees were $2.3 billion, down 5%, driven by lower fees across all products.
